title: Synchronizing program presentation
abstract: Systems and methods that may facilitate synchronizing the presentation of a program on multiple devices are disclosed. Amounts by which each device may delay its presentation of a program may be determined and/or sent to the devices.
claims: 1. A method comprising: receiving, by a computing device: a first audio fingerprint of content from a first user device; a first time value indicating when a position, within the content, was received at the first user device; a second audio fingerprint of the content from a second user device; and a second time value indicating when the position was received at the second user device; determining, based on the first time value and the second time value, an alignment of the first audio fingerprint and the second audio fingerprint by shifting: the first audio fingerprint, from an original position of the first audio fingerprint, within a maximum distance, and based on the first audio fingerprint being shifted the maximum distance without achieving the alignment, the second audio fingerprint; determining, based on the alignment, delay information associated with facilitating synchronization of a first output of the content by the first user device with a second output of the content by the second user device; and sending, to the first user device or the second user device, the delay information.
